name: FieldOps Design System
philosophy: FieldOps is built for technicians who work with their hands, not at desks. Every decision in this system serves one user in one moment: a gloved hand holding a phone in a dim equipment room, needing to log a diagnosis and move on. The palette is earthy — warm neutrals drawn from terracotta, clay, and sage — because field work is material work. Typography runs three registers: Literata carries editorial weight at display sizes for status moments and headings; Manrope handles all functional UI and body text with humanist clarity; Geist Mono locks in precision for every ID, ETA, timestamp, and code. Spacing is generous at touch targets and disciplined at data density. Offline is the default state, not an exception — the system never communicates absence, only action. Nothing here decorates; everything informs.

primitives.name: neutral-50
primitives.usage: Warmest canvas surface — barely-there warmth, not beige

primitives.name: neutral-900
primitives.usage: Near-black for top-priority content, nav backgrounds

primitives.name: clay-200
primitives.usage: Clay subtle border, icon tint in error states

primitives.name: clay-500
primitives.usage: Primary brand accent — terracotta clay, critical/urgent, destructive CTA

primitives.name: clay-700
primitives.usage: Clay pressed state, critical dark text on clay-50

primitives.name: sage-700
primitives.usage: Sage pressed state, success dark text on sage-50

primitives.name: amber-700
primitives.usage: Amber pressed state, warning dark text on amber-50

primitives.name: white
primitives.usage: Pure white — signature canvas, modals, top cards needing maximum contrast

semantic.name: color-canvas
semantic.usage: Page/screen background — the warm ground everything sits on

semantic.name: color-surface
semantic.usage: Card and panel surface — sits above canvas

semantic.name: color-surface-raised
semantic.usage: Raised surface inside a card (section headers, grouped fields)

semantic.name: color-surface-sunken
semantic.usage: Pressed/hover state, read-only input backgrounds

semantic.name: color-nav-bg
semantic.usage: Bottom nav bar background — dark anchors the screen

semantic.name: color-text-primary
semantic.usage: Primary body text, headings on canvas/surface

semantic.name: color-text-secondary
semantic.usage: Supporting text, field labels, secondary data

semantic.name: color-text-muted

semantic.name: color-text-inverse
semantic.usage: Text on dark surfaces — nav bar, primary CTA buttons

semantic.name: color-text-on-clay
semantic.usage: Text on clay-500 backgrounds — badge labels, button labels

semantic.name: color-text-on-sage
semantic.usage: Text on sage-500 backgrounds — confirmed/success button labels

semantic.name: color-border
semantic.usage: Default border for inputs, cards, dividers

semantic.name: color-border-strong
semantic.usage: Focused input border, active tab underline

semantic.name: color-border-focus
semantic.usage: Focused field ring — brand clay pulls attention to active input

semantic.name: color-interactive
semantic.usage: Primary interactive color — buttons, links, active indicators

semantic.name: color-interactive-hover
semantic.usage: Hover/pressed state of primary interactive

semantic.name: color-status-active
semantic.usage: EM CAMPO — technician is on site and working

semantic.name: color-status-active-bg
semantic.usage: EM CAMPO badge background

semantic.name: color-status-transit
semantic.usage: EM TRÂNSITO — technician is en route

semantic.name: color-status-transit-bg
semantic.usage: EM TRÂNSITO badge background

semantic.name: color-status-paused
semantic.usage: PAUSADO — blocked, waiting for material or approval

semantic.name: color-status-paused-bg
semantic.usage: PAUSADO badge background

semantic.name: color-status-done
semantic.usage: CONCLUÍDO — closed orders, de-emphasized

semantic.name: color-status-done-bg
semantic.usage: CONCLUÍDO badge background

semantic.name: color-status-urgent
semantic.usage: URGENTE accent bar on OrderCard — darker clay for maximum urgency signal

semantic.name: color-critical
semantic.usage: Destructive actions, overdue, blocking errors

semantic.name: color-critical-subtle
semantic.usage: Error field backgrounds, inline validation error surfaces

semantic.name: color-success
semantic.usage: Confirmation, step complete, sign-off CTA

semantic.name: color-success-subtle
semantic.usage: Completed step backgrounds, success inline notice

semantic.name: color-warning
semantic.usage: Caution states, connectivity warn, at-risk timers

semantic.name: color-warning-subtle
semantic.usage: Warning inline notice background

semantic.name: color-offline
semantic.usage: OfflineIndicator text — calm, not alarming, saving locally

semantic.name: color-syncing
semantic.usage: OfflineIndicator syncing state — warm activity signal

semantic.name: color-progress-fill
semantic.usage: GuidedStep progress bar fill — brand clay shows advancement

semantic.name: color-progress-track
semantic.usage: GuidedStep progress bar track background

typeScale.name: display-2xl
typeScale.usage: PauseHero heading (OS PAUSADA), large numeric data moments on dashboard KPI cards

typeScale.name: display-xl
typeScale.usage: Screen-level status announcement, major step transitions — only one per screen

typeScale.name: display-lg
typeScale.usage: Section hero headings, GuidedStep screen title, card modal headers

typeScale.name: heading-lg
typeScale.usage: Top-level screen headings (Lista de Ordens, Diagnóstico), sidebar group headers

typeScale.name: heading-md
typeScale.usage: Card primary identifier (customer name, OS title), section subheadings

typeScale.name: heading-sm
typeScale.usage: Card secondary title, sub-section labels, form field group headers

typeScale.name: label-lg
typeScale.usage: Primary CTA button text, major action labels — 56px+ touch target buttons

typeScale.name: label-md
typeScale.usage: Status badge labels (EM CAMPO, PAUSADO), tab pill labels, secondary button text, field labels

typeScale.name: label-sm
typeScale.usage: Metadata micro-labels (PASSO 2 DE 4, count bubbles), uppercase step annotations

typeScale.name: body-lg
typeScale.usage: Textarea content (diagnosis notes), form input text — 16px minimum prevents iOS auto-zoom

typeScale.name: body-md
typeScale.usage: Card body content (address, service description, activity log entries), checklist item text

typeScale.name: body-sm
typeScale.usage: Supporting metadata, SummaryCard data rows, notification banner body, timestamp strings

typeScale.name: mono-lg
typeScale.usage: PauseHero elapsed timer display, large OS number/ID in hero moments

typeScale.name: mono-md
typeScale.usage: TimerStrip running time, ETA values on OrderCard, OS IDs, sync progress counts

typeScale.name: mono-sm
typeScale.usage: Inline technical codes, part numbers, coordinates, small timestamp badges

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.usage: Resting OrderCards, SummaryCard, FormField inputs on canvas — barely-there lift, keeps cards scannable without heaviness

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.usage: GuidedStep card container, raised panels in dashboard, active/focused input state ring shadow supplement

shadows.name: shadow-lg
shadows.usage: Bottom sheet overlays, SignatureCanvas container, PauseHero card, OfflineIndicator when escalated to inline notice

components.name: StatusBadge
variants: em-campo/md: sage-50 bg, sage-500 text, label-md SemiBold, 4px h-pad, 2px v-pad, radius-full, optional sage-500 dot 6px
em-campo/sm: same palette, label-sm, condensed padding 3px/1px
em-transito/md: amber-50 bg, amber-700 text, amber-500 dot
em-transito/sm: same palette, label-sm
pausado/md: clay-50 bg, clay-700 text, clay-500 dot
pausado/sm: same palette, label-sm
concluido/md: neutral-150 bg, neutral-600 text, neutral-400 dot
concluido/sm: same palette, label-sm
urgente/md: clay-500 bg, white text, no dot — filled pill for maximum urgency signal
offline/sm: neutral-200 bg, neutral-600 text — appears in OfflineIndicator slot only
components.description: Color-coded status pill used in OrderCards, PauseHero, and nav. Always pairs semantic color fill or border with a short label in label-md/sm SemiBold — never color alone, never label alone. Minimum height 24px (md), 20px (sm). Dot indicator is 6px circle, matches text color. md variant minimum touch target: not independently tappable — sits inside a card. All text must clear 4.5:1 on their background; clay-700 on clay-50 clears 7:1 for outdoor use.

components.name: Button
variants: primary/default: clay-500 bg, white label-lg, radius-md, height 56px, full-width on mobile, horizontal pad space-6
primary/hover: clay-700 bg, white label-lg — darker clay on press
primary/pressed: clay-700 bg, shadow-sm inset 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)
primary/disabled: neutral-300 bg, neutral-500 text, no interaction
primary-success/default: sage-500 bg, white label-lg, height 56px — for Confirmar Fechamento CTA only
primary-success/hover: sage-700 bg
secondary/default: white bg, clay-500 border 1.5px, clay-500 label-lg, height 52px, radius-md
secondary/hover: clay-50 bg, clay-500 border
secondary/disabled: neutral-100 bg, neutral-300 border, neutral-400 text
ghost/default: transparent bg, no border, clay-500 label-md, height 44px minimum — for inline actions like Re-sign
ghost/hover: clay-50 bg, radius-sm
destructive/default: white bg, clay-500 border 1.5px, clay-700 label-md, height 48px — Cancel OS, Reschedule
destructive/hover: clay-50 bg
destructive/pressed-confirm: clay-500 bg, white label-md — second tap needed; button state change IS the first confirmation step
icon-button: 44x44px minimum tap target, icon 20x20px centered, ghost style, radius-md
components.description: All primary CTAs are full-width on mobile at 56px height (space-14) — gloved-thumb safe. Primary and primary-success use clay-500 and sage-500 respectively as the sole color-personality buttons. Destructive actions require two-tap confirmation: first tap transitions button to clay-500 filled state, second tap executes — this positional shift (label changes, color fills) is the confirmation mechanism, not an alert dialog. Minimum tap target for all buttons: 44px height (space-12) with invisible extension if visual is smaller. Letter spacing on label-lg at button size: 0.005em.

components.name: OrderCard
variants: standard: white bg, shadow-sm, radius-lg, 16px pad (space-4), 4px left accent bar in status color
urgent: clay-700 4px left accent bar, urgente StatusBadge top-right, otherwise identical structure
in-transit: amber-500 4px left accent bar, em-transito StatusBadge
active: sage-500 4px left accent bar, em-campo StatusBadge
paused: clay-500 4px left accent bar, pausado StatusBadge
pressed/active-state: neutral-150 bg, shadow removed — feedback on tap
skeleton: three neutral-200 placeholder bars at exact row heights, single 2s opacity pulse animation
components.description: The central mobile scanning unit. Strict three-data-point discipline: (1) StatusBadge top-right + left accent bar — status readable in 0.5s; (2) heading-md customer name + body-sm address below — identity readable in 0.5s; (3) mono-md ETA or scheduled time bottom-right — timing readable in 0.5s. Nothing else surfaces at the list level. Full card height: 88px minimum, 96px comfortable. Tap target is the entire card. 8px vertical gap between cards. Status accent bar is 4px wide, full card height, flush left, radius-sm on right edge only. The eye path is deterministic: top-left (name) → top-right (status) → bottom-right (time).

components.name: FormField
variants: text-input/default: white bg, neutral-200 border 1px, radius-md, height 52px, body-lg text, label above (body-sm SemiBold label-md color), space-2 gap label-to-input
text-input/focused: clay-500 border 1.5px, shadow-md supplemental glow rgba(181,92,62,0.08)
text-input/filled: neutral-200 border, body-lg clay-800 text
text-input/error: clay-500 border 1.5px, clay-50 bg, clay-700 body-sm error message below with clay-500 icon 14px
text-input/disabled: neutral-100 bg, neutral-300 border, neutral-400 text, no interaction
text-input/required: label has clay-500 asterisk suffix — required fields always labeled, never discovered on submit
textarea/default: same as text-input but min-height 120px, resize none on mobile, body-lg text
textarea/focused: clay-500 border 1.5px

textarea/conditional-hidden: display:none until trigger field answer matches condition — appears with 0.15s fade-in, no layout shift (conditional fields always reserve space via visibility:hidden before trigger)
checkbox/default: 22x22px touch area visual, 44x44px tap target, neutral-200 border 1.5px, radius-sm, unchecked state
checkbox/checked: clay-500 bg, white checkmark 14px, radius-sm
checkbox/disabled: neutral-100 bg, neutral-300 border
checklist-item/default: horizontal row — checkbox left, body-md text, space-3 gap, full-width tap target 48px height
checklist-item/checked: body-md text neutral-400 (de-emphasis), checkbox checked state
photo-upload-tile/empty: neutral-100 bg, neutral-300 border 1px dashed, radius-md, 80x80px, camera icon neutral-400 20px centered
photo-upload-tile/filled: thumbnail image, radius-md, remove affordance: clay-500 circle 20px with white X top-right corner
photo-upload-tile/add: neutral-200 bg, clay-500 plus icon 20px — appears after all fill slots used
components.description: Labels always sit above inputs, never floating inside — floating labels fail when field is filled and label visually merges with value. Input height 52px minimum for glove clearance (4px above Apple's 48px minimum). Font size 16px minimum in all inputs to prevent iOS auto-zoom. Required fields show clay-500 asterisk at label level — technicians discover requirements before they touch the field, not after. Conditional fields fade in at 150ms, never cause layout jump. Error states show red-family (clay) border + background tint + message below — never just color alone. Photo grid uses 2-column layout inside GuidedStep card; each tile 80px square.

components.name: ProgressStep
variants: progress-bar/default: neutral-200 track, clay-500 fill, height 4px, radius-full, width 100% of parent
progress-bar/complete: sage-500 fill — final step or all steps done
step-dot/incomplete: 8px circle, neutral-300 fill
step-dot/current: 10px circle, clay-500 fill, white 4px inner dot — size increase signals active
step-dot/complete: 8px circle, sage-500 fill, white check 6px
step-counter/label: label-sm Manrope SemiBold, neutral-500 color, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.04em — 'PASSO 2 DE 4'
step-title/label: heading-md or display-lg Manrope Bold, color-text-primary — the current step name
full-header-group: step-counter label + step-title + progress bar + dot row — stacked with space-2 gaps, full bleed top of GuidedStep screen
components.description: The GuidedStep header communicates two things simultaneously: absolute position (Passo 2 de 4) and relative progress (bar fill at 50%). Step dots provide at-a-glance waypoint map. The progress bar is 4px tall — enough to be scannable, not so thick it dominates. Dot row sits below the bar with space-2 gap. Current dot is 10px vs 8px for inactive — the size delta is the active signal, not just color. Complete dots turn sage to distinguish done-steps from upcoming steps. The entire header group has 16px left/right margin, 20px top padding from status bar, 16px bottom padding before card content.

components.name: NavigationBar
variants: top-bar/standard: white bg, height 56px, back-chevron ghost icon-button left (44x44), heading-md title center, optional right action ghost icon-button
top-bar/transparent: no bg, title and icons in white — used over PauseHero dark sections
bottom-nav/default: neutral-900 bg, height 64px + safe-area-bottom, horizontal flex with space-between, 4 icon+label slots
bottom-nav/item-active: clay-500 icon + label-sm clay-500 text
bottom-nav/item-inactive: neutral-500 icon + label-sm neutral-500 text
bottom-nav/offline-slot: replaces one nav slot or sits as a persistent strip above nav icons — OfflineIndicator component
status-bar-area: 44px height reserved, no custom content — system status bar passthrough
components.description: Top nav bar uses white surface with shadow-sm to separate from canvas. Back arrow is always a ghost icon-button with 44x44 tap target. Title uses heading-md centered. Right action slot optional — used for filter (Lista de Ordens) or overflow menu. Bottom nav is dark (neutral-900) anchoring the screen spatially — primary actions belong here per thumb-zone rule. Nav icons are 24x24px with 44px tap targets. The OfflineIndicator lives either as a persistent strip above the nav items or replaces the least-used slot — implementation choice, but it must always be visible when offline/syncing without overlapping primary actions.

components.name: TabChip
variants: active: clay-500 bg, white label-md SemiBold, radius-full, height 36px, h-pad space-4, optional white count bubble
inactive: neutral-100 bg, neutral-600 label-md, radius-full, height 36px, h-pad space-4, optional neutral-500 count bubble
inactive/hover: neutral-150 bg
count-bubble/active: white bg, clay-500 label-sm — sits right of label with space-1 gap
count-bubble/inactive: neutral-200 bg, neutral-600 label-sm
tab-row: horizontal scroll, space-2 gap between chips, 16px left-pad, 8px top/bottom pad, white bg or canvas bg
components.description: Filter tabs for job queue (Todas / Em Campo / Em Trânsito / Pausadas). Active tab uses filled clay-500 — the brand accent signals the user's current view clearly. Count bubbles sit inline right of the label, not as superscript badges, keeping the layout stable when counts change. Tab row is horizontally scrollable on mobile — do not truncate labels. Minimum chip width: 68px. Height: 36px with 44px invisible tap target via top/bottom padding. Chips flow left to right in frequency order: Todas first, then status-specific.

components.name: OfflineIndicator
variants: offline/default: neutral-500 icon (cloud with down-slash 16px) + body-sm 'Salvando localmente' neutral-600 text — calm, no alarm
syncing/default: amber-500 rotating upload icon 16px + body-sm 'Enviando...' amber-700 text — warm activity signal
reconnecting/default: neutral-400 pulsing dot 8px + body-sm 'Reconectando...' neutral-500 text — minimal, no anxiety
all states: horizontal flex row, space-1 gap icon-to-text, height 28px, fits in nav or strip above nav
escalated/inline-notice: InlineNotice component activated only when a specific GuidedStep requires real-time validation — this is separate from the always-visible nav indicator
components.description: The OfflineIndicator communicates what IS happening, not what is absent. Never uses red. Never says 'Sem conexão' or 'Offline'. The three states use: neutral tone for saving locally (calm, trustworthy), amber for active upload (warm activity), muted neutral for reconnecting (unhurried). The escalation to InlineNotice only fires when a specific step gates on real-time validation — this is rare. In normal offline use, the nav indicator is the sole communication channel. Icon size 16px, text body-sm. The full indicator component sits in the BottomNav above the icon row or replaces a secondary nav slot.

components.name: Avatar
variants: initials/sm: 28px circle, neutral-200 bg, neutral-700 label-sm — for activity log entries
initials/md: 36px circle, clay-50 bg, clay-700 label-sm SemiBold — technician assignment on OrderCard detail
initials/lg: 48px circle, clay-500 bg, white label-md SemiBold — technician header in PauseHero detail card
initials/xl: 64px circle, clay-500 bg, white heading-md Bold — profile or user switcher context
photo/md: 36px circle, image fill, clay-500 border 1.5px — when photo is available
photo/lg: 48px circle, image fill, clay-500 border 2px
online-dot: 8px circle, sage-500 fill, white border 2px — bottom-right of avatar when technician is reachable
offline-dot: 8px circle, neutral-400 fill, white border 2px
components.description: Avatars represent technicians in dispatch view, detail cards, and activity logs. Initials variant uses two-character uppercase from name. Color palette for initials backgrounds can rotate across a set of warm-tinted primitives (clay-50, amber-50, sage-50, neutral-150) to differentiate multiple technicians at a glance — clay-500 is reserved for the primary/self technician. Minimum touch target if tappable: 44px with invisible extension. Tap reveals technician detail sheet (bottom sheet, not navigation).

components.name: SignatureCanvas
variants: empty/default: white bg, neutral-200 border 1px, radius-md, full usable width (calc(100% - 32px)), height 200px minimum, baseline guide (neutral-200 1px line at 75% height), body-sm neutral-400 italic instruction text 'Assine acima' centered above baseline
has-strokes: same container, strokes rendered in neutral-900 at 2px weight, smooth SVG paths — no jagged pixel rendering
re-sign: ghost Button below canvas, label 'Limpar e assinar novamente', clay-500 text, 44px height, full width — never an X on the canvas itself
confirmed: sage-50 bg, sage-500 border 1.5px, sage-500 checkmark 20px top-right corner of container
error/unsigned: clay-500 border 1.5px, clay-50 bg, 'Assinatura obrigatória' body-sm clay-700 below canvas
landscape-hint: optional body-sm neutral-500 hint text 'Gire o dispositivo para uma área maior' — appears only if viewport height < 600px
components.description: SignatureCanvas is always a GuidedStep — it appears sequenced after the last documentation step, never launched by a button mid-flow. Full usable width: 100% of screen minus 32px horizontal margin (16px each side). Minimum height 200px; 240px preferred. Baseline guide at 75% canvas height gives the signing hand a reference line without constraining it. The Re-sign affordance is a clearly labeled ghost Button below the canvas — the word 'Limpar' makes the action explicit and recoverable. Stroke rendering uses smooth path interpolation (Catmull-Rom or similar) — raw touchmove coordinates produce jagged lines. Canvas listens for touchstart/touchmove/touchend; no stylus pressure required but pressure data can weight stroke width if available.
